title: Efficient external call returndata bubbling
explanation: Directly evaluates the boolean return from `call`. If `0` (revert), it immediately checks `returndatasize()`. If returndata exists, it copies it back to memory using `returndatacopy` and directly passes it to `revert()`, perfectly preserving the original error signature and message with zero overhead logic.
when_to_apply: Any time an external hook or safe transfer callback is executed.
when_not_to_apply: When you specifically need to catch and suppress the revert rather than bubbling it up to the user.
